name: Housing Resources, Inc-Racine
description_short: Non-profit housing counselor providing free homebuyer education, down payment grants, and mortgage assistance across Wisconsin counties including Racine.
description_long:
Housing Resources, Inc. was founded in 1991 in the Milwaukee area and has spent over three decades establishing itself as a reliable non-profit dedicated to homeownership education and financial empowerment. The organization primarily serves low and moderate-income families across Milwaukee, Waukesha, Ozaukee, Washington, Racine, Kenosha, and Walworth counties, with a specific focus on first-time homebuyers who might otherwise lack access to professional guidance.

HRI offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services for both prospective and current homeowners. For home buyers, they provide free monthly workshops and classes covering topics like credit management, mortgage loan fundamentals, affordability assessment, and home shopping strategies. Their one-on-one pre-purchase counseling includes personalized guidance on finances, credit correction, loan product selection, and the entire loan closing process. They also assist clients in applying for multiple down payment and closing cost grant programs. For existing homeowners, HRI offers post-purchase counseling, default prevention assistance, reverse mortgage counseling, a tool loan program, minor home repair services, and operates a Racine Financial Empowerment Center.

What distinguishes Housing Resources, Inc. is their deep expertise in accessing grant funding—they help clients navigate over a dozen specific down payment assistance programs including Downpayment Plus®, Chase Homebuyer Grants, and county-specific mortgage assistance initiatives. Their one-on-one counseling model combines financial assessment with practical loan navigation, and they offer complementary credit report preparation services. The organization's long operational history and multi-county service area demonstrate sustainability and established community partnerships.

The primary honest caveat is that while their services are free or low-cost, they cannot guarantee loan approval or grant qualification—they can only counsel and assist applications. Geographic service is limited to specific Wisconsin counties, potentially excluding some residents. Additionally, the website lacks detail on average counseling wait times, success rates for grant applications, or specific default prevention outcomes, making it difficult to assess impact metrics.
pros: Free homebuyer education classes and workshops covering credit, affordability, and mortgage fundamentals
One-on-one pre-purchase counseling with experienced specialists at no cost
Assists with applications for 11+ specific down payment and closing cost grant programs
Serves low and moderate-income families specifically, with no fee barriers to entry
Post-purchase support including default counseling and mortgage assistance programs to prevent foreclosure
Offers practical ancillary services like tool loans and minor home repair assistance for homeowners
Established non-profit with 30+ year track record (founded 1991) across seven Wisconsin counties
cons: Service area limited to seven Wisconsin counties only—not available nationwide or in all states
Website does not disclose average counseling wait times, application turnaround, or success rates for grant approvals
Grant qualification and loan approval ultimately depend on lender and program requirements; HRI cannot guarantee outcomes
No information on whether reverse mortgage counseling or default assistance requires HUD certification verification
Limited transparency on staffing credentials, counselor certifications, or whether they are NFCC-certified
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Wisconsin counties seeking free education and down payment grant assistance
Low to moderate-income families who need personalized credit and affordability counseling before mortgage application
Current homeowners facing foreclosure risk or seeking post-purchase financial coaching and mortgage assistance
Borrowers with credit challenges who need structured guidance on credit repair and loan product selection
diagnosis: Housing Resources, Inc. is best for low and moderate-income first-time homebuyers and struggling homeowners in Wisconsin counties (especially Racine) who need free education, personalized counseling, and help accessing down payment grants or foreclosure prevention assistance. The main caveat is that service is geographically limited to seven specific Wisconsin counties and grant/loan approval ultimately depends on third-party lenders and program eligibility—HRI can counsel and assist but cannot guarantee outcomes.
services: Home Buyer Classes and Monthly Workshops
Down Payment and Closing Cost Grant Application Assistance
Mortgage Assistance Programs (Milwaukee County, City of Racine, Waukesha County)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
